perItem.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください
読めば読むほど面白い
今日は休むわけにはいきません
今日は来なくてもいいです
知らぬ人に声をかけられた
本当のことを言うべきだ

proposals.title: Complete the Godan Volitional rows to include trailing う (飲も → 飲もう)
proposals.rationale: GodanConjugationMap returns only the お-row mora (む→も, う→お, く→こ, …) for Volitional, while the combine logic at verb-types.d.ts:264 is plain `${stem}${conjugation}`. So ConjugateVerb<飲む,'Volitional'> = 飲も, not 飲もう, forcing every godan volitional to hand-append a raw literal う. The ichidan branch (よう) and irregular map (しよう/来よう/こよう) ARE complete, so the godan path is the inconsistent outlier. This is the single highest-frequency ts-def gap: it breaks D4 (conjugator must produce the full 意向形) and D6 (forces 飲も+う mis-segmentation) for EVERY godan volitional, not just b2-5.
proposals.change: In src/verb-types.d.ts GodanConjugationMap, append う to every Volitional value: う→"おう", く→"こう", ぐ→"ごう", す→"そう", つ→"とう", ぬ→"のう", ぶ→"ぼう", む→"もう", る→"ろう". No change to the combine logic needed; ConjugateVerb<飲む,'Volitional'> then resolves to 飲もう directly. Update the example comment at example-verb.ts:45 (買うVolitional) accordingly.

proposals.title: Stop listing 早く / 本当に as Adverb examples; route adjective 連用形 to Adverbial conjugation
proposals.rationale: annotate.ts:133 gives `Adverb<Word> // 副詞: 毎日, 早く, すぐ, また, 本当に` — but 早く is the 連用形 (く) of i-adjective 早い and 本当に is the に form of na-adjective 本当, so the prompt is actively teaching the model to lexicalize inflected adjectives as base adverbs. This directly causes the D1 POS error and D4 violation flagged on b2-6, and a chapter survey shows the same mistake recurring (早く×4, よく×1, 本当に×1, 一緒に×2 wrapped in Adverb). The library already exposes ConjugateAdjective Adverbial (い→く, な→に), so these are fully modellable and should be derived.
proposals.change: In annotate.ts API_REFERENCE line 133, change the Adverb examples to genuine lexical adverbs only (e.g. 毎日, すぐ, また, とても, ずっと) and DROP 早く/本当に. Add one hard rule under # Hard rules: 'An adjective in its 連用形 (i-adj く: 早く/高く/よく; na-adj に: 本当に/上手に/静かに) is NOT a lexical 副詞 — model it as AdjectivePart<A,"Adverbial">, never AdverbPart. Reserve Adverb/AdverbPart for words with no adjective base (毎日, すぐ, また).'

proposals.title: Add a negative te-form (Nakute) so 〜なくて(も) is conjugator-derived, not a literal
proposals.rationale: ConjugateVerb has no negative-te form. For 来なくてもいい (b2-9) ConjugateVerb<来る,'Nai'> returns only the bare 未然形 stem 来, so the author must hand-write the literal なくても — collapsing three morphemes (なく/て/も) into one leaf and burying the permission-construction particle も. This breaks D2, D3, and D4 simultaneously and recurs in any 〜なくてもいい / 〜なくては / 〜なければ permission/obligation pattern. A first-class Nakute form lets 来なくて be a single VerbPart, exposing も as its own ParticlePart.
proposals.change: In src/verb-types.d.ts: add "Nakute" to ConjugationForm; in ConjugateVerb resolve it as `${ConjugateVerb<V,"Nai">}なくて` for godan/ichidan/irregular (note ichidan/irregular 'Nai' already returns the bare stem 来/食べ, so 来 + なくて = 来なくて; godan 'Nai' returns the あ-row stem 行か, so 行か + なくて needs the い→ない→なくて path, i.e. append ない's なくて as `${naiStem}なくて`). Then update the prompt to use VerbPart<V,"Nakute"> + ParticlePart<"も"> for the 〜なくてもいい pattern.

proposals.title: Complete Godan/Ichidan Hypothetical to the え+ば conditional form (読め → 読めば)
proposals.rationale: Hypothetical returns only the bare 仮定形 stem (godan え-row 読め; ichidan 読められ is also off — see note), so 読めば (b2-7) must hand-append the literal ば. Parallel to the Volitional gap: the conjugator stops one mora short of the real inflected surface, forcing a raw-literal particle that should be a typed unit. Lower frequency than Volitional but the same class of fix.
proposals.change: In src/verb-types.d.ts, change the godan Hypothetical row values from the bare え-row mora to include ば (う→"えば", く→"けば", ぐ→"げば", す→"せば", つ→"てば", ぬ→"ねば", ぶ→"べば", む→"めば", る→"れば"); for ichidan set Hypothetical to `${stem}れば`; for irregular set すれ→"すれば", 来れ→"来れば", くれ→"くれば". (Note: the current ichidan branch lumps Hypothetical with Potential/Passive as ${stem}られ, which is wrong even ignoring ば — split it out.)

proposals.title: Fix b2-2 行きますが遅れます: expose the contrastive が as a particle, not a literal
proposals.rationale: The 接続助詞 が ('but') is hardcoded as a literal between two ConjugateVerb results (D3 high). が is already in the Particle union, so even without a clause-joining type it can be a visible ParticlePart. Multiple judges split on whether the deeper gap (no clause-conjunction construct) is ts-def, but the immediate, safe fix is content: make が a ParticlePart so particle integrity is restored.
proposals.change: Rewrite the b2-2 snippet as Sentence<[VerbPart<行く,"Masu">, ParticlePart<"が">, VerbPart<遅れる,"Masu">]> so the contrastive が is its own ParticlePart instead of raw template-literal glue. (Optional follow-up: a clause-conjunction type in ts-def, but not required for this fix.)

proposals.title: Fix b2-10 知らぬ人に声をかけられた: derive the passive past from the conjugator
proposals.rationale: かけられた is a flat literal hiding stem+passive+past (かけ/られ/た) — the grammatical focus of the sentence — even though かける is a plain ichidan verb the library fully supports (Passive = ${stem}られ). This is a clear authoring miss (D2/D4 high), not a library gap; the classical ぬ on 知らぬ is correctly left literal per the rubric allowance.
proposals.change: In the b2-10 snippet declare かける as IchidanVerb & { stem:"かけ"; ending:"る" } and replace the literal かけられた with VerbPart<かける,"Passive"> + SuffixPart<"た"> (or a Passive+Ta path), so the passive and past are conjugator-derived and かける gets a visible verb-class assertion.

proposals.title: Fix b2-11 本当のことを言うべきだ: surface の as a ParticlePart and だ as a CopulaPart
proposals.rationale: The linking 助詞 の is buried in `${本当}の${こと}` (D3 high) and the copula だ is folded into the べきだ literal (D4). Both are modellable: の is in the Particle union and だ has Copula<"Plain">. Only the classical べき (連体形 of べし) legitimately stays a literal per the rubric. Pure content fix.
proposals.change: Rewrite b2-11 as Sentence<[NounPart<"本当">, ParticlePart<"の">, NounPart<"こと">, ParticlePart<"を">, VerbPart<言う,"Dictionary">, SuffixPart<"べき">, CopulaPart<"Plain">]> so の is a ParticlePart, だ is a CopulaPart, and only べき remains a (sanctioned classical) SuffixPart.

issues.note: Uses raw template-literal interpolation (PhraseWithParticle/ConjugateCopula) rather than the structured Sentence<[PronounPart, ParticlePart, NounPart, CopulaPart]> form the library also offers. Grammatically correct and every morpheme is a visible typed unit, but the Part-based form would surface explicit POS tags. Style preference only; no dimension is harmed.
issues.jp: 私は学生です

issues.note: が (contrastive conjunctive particle, ~けど 'but') is hardcoded as a literal inside the template literal instead of being expressed via the Particle type / PhraseWithParticle. The library defines が in the Particle union, so this is an authoring omission, not a library gap.
issues.jp: 行きますが遅れます

issues.note: Two clauses are concatenated with a raw が glue; idiomatic modelling would treat 行きますが as a phrase-with-particle (or use a clause-connector) so the contrastive particle is structurally visible.
issues.jp: 行きますが遅れます

issues.note: The connecting 接続助詞 が ('but', contrastive clause-linker) is emitted as a bare literal between two ConjugateVerb results. There is no clause-joining construct in the library to compose two predicates with a conjunctive particle, so the が cannot be made a first-class typed unit; PhraseWithParticle attaches a particle to one phrase but does not join a following clause.
issues.jp: 行きますが遅れます

issues.note: Even within current library limits, 行きますが could have been expressed as PhraseWithParticle<ConjugateVerb<行く,'Masu'>, 'が'> to make the particle a visible ParticlePart, instead of a raw literal in the template.
issues.jp: 行きますが遅れます

issues.note: The natural teacher segmentation is 行きます / が / 遅れます; without a sentence-level compound/conjunction type the snippet falls back to literal concatenation, lowering idiomatic fidelity.
issues.jp: 行きますが遅れます

issues.note: The conjunctive/contrastive particle が ("but") is embedded as a raw literal between the two clauses. There is no dedicated Particle/Conjunction constructor to make it a typed unit, so it is technically a visible but untyped span. Acceptable but not fully modeled.
issues.jp: 行きますが遅れます

issues.note: Minor style only: i-adjective declared as `IAdjective & { stem; ending }` intersection rather than the library's canonical object-literal pattern `{ type: "i-adjective"; stem; ending }`. Type-equivalent (intersection carries the required type:"i-adjective" field) and grammatically correct; no impact on analysis.
issues.jp: 昨日は寒かった

issues.note: 寒い is declared as `IAdjective & { stem; ending }` rather than the library's idiomatic object form with a `type: "i-adjective"` discriminant (cf. examples in adjective-types.d.ts). It type-checks because IAdjective has no required discriminant, but it deviates from convention; purely cosmetic.
issues.jp: 昨日は寒かった

issues.note: 昨日 is modeled as CommonNoun; some grammarians treat time words like 昨日 as adverbial/time nouns (副詞的名詞). The library offers no finer subclass, so CommonNoun is the best available and remains defensible.
issues.jp: 昨日は寒かった

issues.note: GetGodanConjugation maps Volitional for む to just "も" (the お-row kana) without the trailing う, unlike the ichidan branch which returns the full "よう". This forces the snippet to append う as a raw literal, so the conjugator does not fully model the 意向形. The library godan Volitional row should include う (e.g. "もう") to make 飲もう fully conjugator-derived.
issues.jp: コーヒーを飲もう

issues.note: Because of the same incomplete godan table, the idiomatic single inflection 飲もう is split into ConjugateVerb<...>+"う", which is not how a teacher segments the volitional. Fixing the table would let the snippet drop the literal う.
issues.jp: コーヒーを飲もう

issues.note: GetGodanConjugation maps Volitional to the o-row mora only (む→も), so ConjugateVerb<飲む,'Volitional'> yields 飲も, not 飲もう. The long-vowel う must be hand-appended as a literal, meaning the conjugator does not fully model the godan volitional. Note ichidan Volitional (${stem}よう) IS complete, so the godan path is inconsistent/incomplete.
issues.jp: コーヒーを飲もう

issues.note: Because of the ts-def gap, 飲もう is segmented as 飲も + literal う rather than as one inflected unit; not how a teacher segments. Fixing the godan Volitional map to include う would let the snippet drop the trailing literal.
issues.jp: コーヒーを飲もう

issues.note: GetGodanConjugation maps Volitional for む to "も" only (verb-types.d.ts:146), so ConjugateVerb<飲む,"Volitional"> = "飲も" not "飲もう". The library cannot express the full Godan volitional; the snippet must append a raw literal "う". The conjugator should append the お-row mora + う.
issues.jp: コーヒーを飲もう

issues.note: The hanging literal "う" breaks the verb into 飲も + う, which no teacher would segment. This is a direct consequence of the incomplete Godan Volitional in the type def rather than an authoring choice.
issues.jp: コーヒーを飲もう

issues.note: 早く is treated as Adverb<"早く"> but it is the adverbial (連用形/く) form of i-adjective 早い; should be derived from IAdjective 早い via Adverbial conjugation.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: The 助詞 に in ように is hidden inside the literal 'ように'; the particle is not a visible unit. よう (formal noun 様) + に should be separated.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: ように collapses two morphemes (よう + に) into one literal leaf.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: 早く not produced by the conjugator; i-adj adverbial く must come from the conjugator per rubric D4.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: ください could in principle model くださる honorific imperative; left as a fixed literal — acceptable as a fixed request auxiliary but library offers no auxiliary type to express it cleanly.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: 早く is treated as Adverb<"早く">, but it is the 連用形 (く-form) of the i-adjective 早い. POS is wrong; it is an inflected adjective, not a lexical adverb.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: Library exposes ConjugateAdjective with Adverbial form (い->く yields 早く); the snippet hardcodes the inflection as a literal instead of deriving it from 早い via the conjugator.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: The particle に in ように is buried in the literal 'ように'. The よう (様/formal noun) + に (particle) structure is hidden as a raw string.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: 'ように' is a partial collapsed literal mixing the よう nominal and the に particle; could be split to expose internal structure.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: 早く is encoded as Adverb<"早く">, collapsing the i-adjective 早い + Adverbial く inflection into a leaf. The library exposes ConjugateAdjective with an "Adverbial" form (く), so this inflection is modellable and should be derived rather than lexicalized. Recurring convention across a02/a07/e17, so it is a prompt/style choice, not a one-off slip.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: ください left as a raw literal. Acceptable since it is the imperative of くださる and conventionally unmodelled, but the chain mixes conjugator-derived して with literal ください; this is fine grammatically and the resolution is correct.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: Strict POS view treats 早く as an inflected i-adjective rather than a base Adverb; the Adverb tag is defensible because 早く is dictionary-listed as an adverb, but it slightly overstates lexical atomicity.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: ConjugateVerb Hypothetical for godan returns only the え-row stem (読め) without ば, so the conditional particle ば must be hand-appended as a raw literal; the library has no full ば-conditional form. Harmless here but the inflection is partly outside the conjugator.
issues.jp: 読めば読むほど面白い

issues.note: 副助詞/formal-noun ほど is a bare template literal with no typed NounPart/particle wrapper; visible but untyped. Acceptable in this pure template-literal style but not modeled as a unit type.
issues.jp: 読めば読むほど面白い

issues.note: ば and ほど are bare string literals rather than typed particle/NounPart units. Conventional and defensible (ば attaches to 仮定形, ほど is a 形式名詞/助詞 appended to 連体形), but a dedicated ParticlePart/NounPart would make the structure more explicit. Library offers no clean way to express ば as a post-仮定形 particle, so this is a definition limitation, not an authoring error.
issues.jp: 読めば読むほど面白い

issues.note: ば is a raw literal because the conjugator exposes only the Hypothetical 仮定形 stem (え-row), not an え+ば form; no library type can render 読めば as a single conjugated unit. Acceptable but worth a ts-def note.
issues.jp: 読めば読むほど面白い

issues.note: ほど (degree formal-noun/particle) is a bare template literal; in template-literal style there is no PronounPart/NounPart wrapper, so it cannot be tagged as a unit. Minor; segmentation itself is correct.
issues.jp: 読めば読むほど面白い

issues.note: The は in わけにはいきません is a real 助詞 (contrastive/topic) but is emitted as a raw literal `は` in the template string instead of a PhraseWithParticle unit. Should be e.g. PhraseWithParticle<..., 'に'> followed by a particle は as its own unit, or a には composite particle, so the particle is visible.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: わけ is a formal/dependent noun (形式名詞) used to nominalize the preceding clause; modeling it as a plain CommonNoun loses that subclass. No dedicated FormalNoun/dependent-noun part appears available in the library.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: わけにはいかない is a fixed grammar construction; the prompt could guide whether to model it compositionally (わけ + には + いく) with には as a single composite particle vs. a literal は, to keep particle integrity.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: The contrastive/topic は in the に+は stack is written as a raw literal string "は" rather than wrapped as a particle. It should be a second PhraseWithParticle (or particle unit), e.g. apply に then は as particles, so the 助詞 is a visible unit.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: わけ is a 形式名詞 (formal/dependent noun) and いく here is a grammaticalized auxiliary within the fixed pattern わけにはいかない; the library has no 形式名詞 class nor a way to mark the idiom, so modeling them as plain CommonNoun/GodanVerb is the best available but loses the fixed-expression nuance.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: わけ analyzed as CommonNoun; a dedicated 形式名詞 subclass would be more precise but none exists.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: The contrastive 助詞 は in わけには is written as a bare literal `は` rather than a visible particle unit (ParticlePart/PhraseWithParticle), burying a genuine particle.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: わけにはいきません is a fixed grammatical idiom meaning 'cannot afford to'; modeling いく as a literal Godan verb is defensible but the whole にはいかない frame is conventionally a unit, and leaving は loose mis-segments it.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: Particle も is buried inside the literal "なくても"; the library offers no negative-te (なくて) form to attach も to as a visible unit, so the 許可・不要 construction cannot expose its particle.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: ConjugateVerb<来る,"Nai"> returns only the negative stem "来" (not 来ない); the actual negative inflection ない→なく plus て is supplied by a raw literal "なくても" rather than the conjugator. No NaiTe/Nakute form exists.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: "なくても" is a single collapsed leaf hiding the negative-adverbial なく, the te-form て, and the particle も.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: Given the missing conjugator support, the snippet leans on Nai+literal; the prompt could have flagged that the 〜なくてもいい permission/non-obligation pattern needs a dedicated negative-te form rather than literal gluing.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: ConjugateVerb<来る,'Nai'> returns only the negative STEM 来, not 来ない. To form the negative te-form 来なくて the author hand-wrote なくても. The conjugator has no 'negative te-form' (NaiTe / Nakute) form, so the negation ending ない and its 連用形 くて plus the も particle are all raw literals instead of being produced by the inflector.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: The particle も in 来なくても (concessive 〜ても) is invisible — embedded in the literal なくても. With no modeled negative-te + も path, it cannot be expressed as a PhraseWithParticle/particle unit.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: Negative auxiliary ない and its inflection (なく+て) are hidden inside a single literal leaf; ideally 来→ない→なくて would be a composed structure.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: 〜なくてもいい is a teachable fixed pattern; the annotate prompt could guide modelling the negative-te explicitly (or, if unmodelable, flagging なくても as a sanctioned whole-pattern literal) rather than using Nai purely for the bare stem.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: ConjugateVerb<来る,"Nai"> returns the bare 未然形 stem "来", not a negative form; the author appended a literal なくても to fake 来なくて. The conjugator is misused to extract a stem, and the negation is raw text.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: も (the permission-construction 助詞 in 〜てもいい) is buried in the literal なくても. There is no library form for the negative te-form (なくて) nor a clean way to expose ても, so part of this is a definitional gap, but も must be a visible particle.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: 来なくても hides ≥3 morphemes (なく/て/も) behind one literal; should decompose into negative-te 来なくて + particle も.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: No 'Nakute' (negative connective) verb form exists, forcing the literal; adding a negative te-form would let 来なくて be conjugated properly.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: いい defined via IAdjective & {stem;ending;irregular} intersection-with-literal rather than the canonical {type:"i-adjective",...} object; resolves but is non-idiomatic authoring.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: かけられた is a flat literal; かける should be typed as IchidanVerb { stem:"かけ"; ending:"る" } and conjugated, exposing stem/passive/past instead of a collapsed leaf.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: Passive should come from ConjugateVerb<かける,"Passive"> (=かけられ) + Ta literal た, not a hardcoded かけられた; the library supports Ichidan Passive (V["stem"]+られ).
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: The main predicate verb かける is absent from the type layer, so the snippet under-models the sentence's core action.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: There is no first-class classical-negative (ぬ/ざる) conjugation form, so 知らぬ must be assembled as Nai-stem + ぬ literal; acceptable but forced by the lib.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: かけられた is a raw literal. かける is an ichidan verb; ConjugateVerb<かける,'Passive'> resolves to 'かけられ' and +'た' gives 'かけられた'. The passive — the grammatical focus of the sentence — must come from the conjugator, not a literal.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: The かけられた leaf hides stem+passive+past (かけ/られ/た). Decompose into ConjugateVerb<かける,'Passive'> + 'た', and declare かける as IchidanVerb.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: かける has no type declaration; the content verb is invisible to POS classification because it lives inside a string literal.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: 知らぬ relies on appending classical negative ぬ as a literal because the library has no classical auxiliary; this is acceptable per the rubric (classical ぬ ok as literal), but the past-passive part had no such excuse.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: かけられた is a raw string literal. The ichidan verb かける supports Passive (`${stem}られ`) and Ta in the library, so it should be ConjugateVerb<かける(IchidanVerb,stem:'かけ'),'Passive'> + 'た' (or a passive-past path), not a literal. Burying the passive in a literal directly violates D4.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: The clause's main predicate (verb + voice + tense) is collapsed into one literal 'かけられた', hiding three morphemes (かけ / られ / た).
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: Because かける is never instantiated as an IchidanVerb, its verb class is asserted nowhere; correct class is ichidan (声をかける, not the godan かかる).
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: 知らぬ correctly treats classical ぬ as an unmodellable literal on the Nai/未然形 stem — this part is idiomatic and well done.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: The linking particle の is written as a string literal inside `${本当}の${こと}` instead of a visible Particle unit; since Particle already includes 'の', this could and should be a typed unit (e.g. PhraseWithParticle<本当,'の'>).
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: だ copula is folded into the raw 'べきだ' literal; there is no Copula/formal-noun constructor for べき+だ, so the bound copula and the べき formal noun are not separately modeled.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べき itself (連体形 of べし) has no library type; treating it as a literal is acceptable, but a べき/formal-noun helper would make the segmentation match a teacher's analysis more closely.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: The 助詞 の is buried in the literal `${本当}の${こと}`; the library has の in the Particle union and a ParticlePart, so a nested PhraseWithParticle (or builder) could expose it as a unit. Annotate guidance should require every particle visible.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べきだ is a single collapsed leaf bundling the formal noun べき and the copula だ; べき is acceptably literal but だ should be a separate modeled morpheme.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: Final だ is hard-coded as a literal rather than emitted by the Copula type (Copula<"Plain">="だ" exists), so the copula is not modelled via the conjugator.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べき (classical 連体形 of べし used as a formal noun) has no dedicated constructor; literal is acceptable per the classical-form allowance, but a SuffixPart/NounPart for べき would let it be a typed unit rather than raw text.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べきだ is a single literal leaf bundling the classical auxiliary べき (rubric-permitted) with the copula だ; だ is independently modelable but no copula constructor is used here, hiding the boundary.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: Copula だ appears as raw text rather than from a copula conjugator; a Copula/だ constructor would let だ be modeled while leaving classical べき as the only literal.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: GodanVerb & {stem;ending} omits the type:"godan" tag; harmless because GodanVerb already requires it and the alias is never instantiated, but it deviates from the canonical {type:"godan";stem;ending} pattern.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ
